name: Potassium
summary: Decision-ready summary: Potassium is best framed for potassium deficiency; blood pressure support; electrolyte balance; safety screen: kidney disease; ACE inhibitors/ARBs; potassium-sparing diuretics.
description: Potassium supports electrolyte balance for normal blood pressure regulation and neuromuscular function; evidence is strongest for deficiency correction and blood pressure outcomes.
safetyNotes: Do not supplement casually with kidney disease or potassium-retaining medications.

claimMap.claim: v10.0: Potassium intake is strongly relevant to BP, but supplement safety requires strict medication/kidney cautions.
claimMap.notes: v10.0: Potassium intake is strongly relevant to BP, but supplement safety requires strict medication/kidney cautions.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Adequate intake: 3400 mg/day adult men, 2600 mg/day adult women; potassium supplements are usually low-dose unless prescribed

sources.title: PubMed PMID 25059961. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
